input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:
`.testsettings` files are **not supported** by modern MSTest — they're silently ignored. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's how your three settings map:

**1. Test timeout** → `[Timeout]` attribute or `.runsettings`
**2. Deployment/assembly resolution** → no longer needed (SDK-style projects handle this)
**3. Code coverage data collector** → `.runsettings` `<DataCollectors>` section

Let me create the replacement `.runsettings` file and update your project:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Global test timeout in milliseconds (was TestTimeout in .testsettings) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- DeploymentEnabled is false by default in SDK-style projects; set true only if needed -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has an MSTest v3 project with a `.testsettings` file. `.testsettings` files are a legacy format that's no longer supported by modern MSTest/VSTest. They need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file.

Let me invoke the migrate-mstest-v1v2-to-v3 skill since this is about migrating from the old `.testsettings` format.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Global test timeout in milliseconds (was TestTimeout in .testsettings) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- DeploymentEnabled is false by default in SDK-style projects; set true only if needed -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content: Now point your project at the new `.runsettings`:

Here's what changed and why:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout></MSTest>` |
| `AssemblyResolution / TestDirectory` | Not needed — SDK-style projects resolve assemblies automatically |
| Code Coverage `DataCollector` | Moved to `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` with updated URI (`2.0`) |

**You can now delete `local.testsettings`** — it has no effect with MSTest v3.

Run tests with: `dotnet test` (the `.runsettings` is picked up automatically via the `RunSettingsFilePath` property). You can also pass it explicitly: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`.
